title: Understanding Bias in Vision
concept: Data bias occurs when training datasets are unrepresentative or imbalanced, causing models to perform poorly on underrepresented groups.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary purpose of red-teaming a vision system?

options.text: To proactively discover model vulnerabilities using deliberate attacks
To increase the training speed of model weights
To convert models to INT8 precision

exercises.explain: Red-teaming simulates real-world attack conditions to find weaknesses before bad actors do.

cards.title: AI is what it eats
cards.body: If you only teach a child what a dog looks like by showing them photos of golden retrievers, they might think a pug is a cat. Neural networks do the same! If our training datasets lack variety, the AI will make biased, inaccurate predictions in the real world.

cards.title: What is Algorithmic Bias?
cards.body: Algorithmic bias occurs when a model systematically performs worse for certain demographic groups or environmental conditions. This is usually caused by data bias: training sets that fail to represent the real-world diversity of users.

cards.title: A skewed textbook library
cards.body: Imagine studying history using only books written in one city. You will have a highly biased understanding of the world. If an AI only studies faces with light skin tones, it will fail to understand and recognize faces with dark skin tones.

cards.title: Facial analysis failures
cards.body: Researchers audited commercial gender classification APIs. They found error rates for light-skinned men were under 1%, but skyrocketed to over 34% for dark-skinned women due to severe underrepresentation in the training datasets.

cards.title: Math can be biased
cards.body: You might think algorithms are neutral because they are just math formulas. In reality, math outputs what the training data input. If the training data reflects human biases or demographic imbalances, the model will learn and lock in those biases.

cards.title: Clean vs. Skewed Datasets
cards.body: How data distributions affect model behavior:

left.text:
Equal representation of groups
Varying skin tones & genders
Consistent accuracy for all users
Fair and robust model

right.text:
One group dominates data (e.g., 90%)
Underrepresented categories
High accuracy overall, poor in groups
Biased model performance

exercises.prompt: What is the primary cause of algorithmic bias in computer vision models?

options.text: Converting weights from FP32 to INT8 precision

exercises.explain: Models learn patterns directly from the training data; if the data is skewed, the model's predictions will be biased.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A model can achieve 95% overall accuracy but still have unacceptable, biased error rates for specific minority groups.
exercises.explain: True. If the majority class dominates 95% of the test set, high overall accuracy can hide a 100% failure rate on the remaining 5%.

exercises.sentence: When a dataset contains way more photos of one group than another, it suffers from data ___.
exercises.explain: Data imbalance occurs when demographic groups are not represented proportionally.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a real-world consequence of biased face recognition systems?

options.text: Higher false arrest rates for underrepresented demographic groups
Faster inference speeds on edge devices
An automatic decrease in model file size

exercises.explain: Biased face recognition leads to high false-match rates, which can result in incorrect identifications in law enforcement.

exercises.prompt: You are building a face-unlock feature for a smartwatch. During testing, you notice the model unlocks 99% of the time for male users, but only 70% of the time for female users. What is the root cause of this issue?

options.text: The training dataset contained significantly fewer photos of female faces, causing poor generalization.
The watch GPU is too slow to process female faces.
The model was exported using CoreML instead of TFLite.
The model has too many horizontal graph fusions.

exercises.explain: Underrepresentation of female faces in the training set prevents the model from learning robust features for that group, creating biased performance.
